meaning: n.[生化]隐黄质,玉米黄质(亦作cryptoxanthol)
examples: It is naturally derived from marigold flowers and contains a normal blend of carotenoids including zeaxanthin and cryptoxanthin.
Similarly, while it is easy to start the day with a glass of orange juice, a handful of papaya contains 15 times more beta-cryptoxanthin - another vitamin A building block.
Carrots, mangoes, apricots, pumpkins, and sweet potatoes are brimming with cryptoxanthin and beta-carotene-two substances that reduce the risk for a variety of mental and physical ailments.
